title: The Kneel
story_reference: Based on story-001-the-arrival.md, scene: the man kneeling at the river before the Guru opens his eyes
hook: A man who had won everything knelt at the edge of a river. He could not name what he had lost.

script.visual: Extreme slow push-in on a man's hands, dirt under the fingernails, a wedding ring catching morning light. Mist rising off a green river. No face yet.
script.voiceover: The man who knelt at the edge of the river had won everything.

script.visual: Jump cut montage, each held 1 second: a magazine cover with his name, a corner office window at dusk, a child's drawing on a fridge, a wife asleep, a ceiling at 3 a.m. No sound but a single low cello note.
script.voiceover: Top of his class. Six hundred employees. A house strangers photographed from the street. A calendar booked three months deep.

script.visual: Return to the riverbank. Wide shot. The man kneeling, small against the water. His shoulders shake once. Stops. Holds still. Birds we cannot name call from the canopy.
script.voiceover: And a hollowness behind the ribs that no achievement could fill. It had started as a whisper. Over the years it had grown teeth.

script.visual: Slow pan to the Guru, seated on a flat stone. Absolutely still. Eyes closed. A leaf falls and lands on his shoulder. He does not move. The light shifts.
script.voiceover: At the other end of the clearing, an old man sat on a stone. Eyes closed. Still as the stone. A leaf fell on his shoulder. He did not move.

script.visual: Back to the kneeling man. His breath visible in the cold air. He looks up for the first time. Tears, quiet. Not performed.
script.voiceover: The man had flown across an ocean. Walked two days along a river. He had not told his office. He had not told himself.

script.visual: Close on the man's face. He whispers something. We do not hear it. The river keeps moving. A bird calls once.
script.voiceover: Among all beings, human birth is rare. Rarer still, the one who wants to know what he is. Rarer than that, the one who kneels.

script.visual: Hold on the river. Black text on bottom third, white serif.

cta: New series. Six scenes from an ancient dialogue. The first one begins here.
caption:
He had won everything. He could not name what he had lost.

Scene one of a six-part narrative series based on the opening of the Vivekachudamani — a 1,200-year-old text in which a disciple arrives at a forest ashram and does not know what he is.

The rarity is not human birth. The rarity is the kneel.

manuṣyatvam
music_suggestion: A single sustained cello note, unresolved. No percussion. Under the montage, one low piano note every four seconds, like a pulse. Fade to only river and birds at 45s. No music at all for the final image.
casting_notes: Two characters: The Seeker (mid-30s to early-40s, exhausted, well-dressed but rumpled — expensive watch on a man who has not slept) and The Guru (older, completely still, minimal movement — his stillness is the performance). Or pure voiceover over landscape B-roll: river at dawn, mist, a kneeling figure shot from behind, an old man's hands on a stone. Zero temple iconography.
